commentary_text: The model favors Croatia at 79% while the sportsbook consensus drops to 58%, a 21-percentage-point gap on the home win that widens further on Ghana, where the model assigns just 3% but the market offers 17%. Both sources agree Croatia is the favorite, but the market substantially hedges that conviction. This divergence could reflect real-time information the model has not yet incorporated—recent lineup changes, injury developments, or unexpected form swings in the lead-up to the match. Alternatively, the market may be pricing in tail risks or applying wider uncertainty bands than the statistical forecast warrants. The dashboard surfaces this as a notable signal: either the model is overconfident in the Croatia advantage, or the market is being unnecessarily cautious.
